preamble: The diphthong /uo/ can undergo a transformation to the close front rounded vowel /y/ primarily through a process of i-umlaut, which first transforms the back rounded diphthong /uo/ into a front rounded diphthong /yø/, followed by monophthongization of /yø/ to /y/.
phoneticEffects: Umlaut (fronting and rounding of a back diphthong), Monophthongization

examples.note: The diphthong /uo/ underwent i-umlaut to /yø/, which subsequently monophthongized to /y:/ in Middle High German. This is exemplified by the shift from forms like *suozi* to *süeze* ('sweet').

sources: Cercignani, F. (2022). The development of the Old High German umlauted vowels and the reflex of New High German /ɛ:/ in Present Standard German. Linguistik Online, 113(1), 45-66.
A Brief History of the German Language - BYU Department of Linguistics.
